diff:
diff --git a/src/preferences.py b/src/preferences.py
index deba08a..bce1399 100644
--- a/src/preferences.py
+++ b/src/preferences.py
@@ -1,10 +1,28 @@
+from copy import deepcopy
+
 def merge_preferences(defaults: dict, overrides: dict) -> dict:
-    merged = defaults.copy()
-    notifications = merged.setdefault("notifications", {})
-    notifications.update(overrides.get("notifications", {}))
-    tags = merged.setdefault("tags", [])
-    tags.extend(overrides.get("tags", []))
+    """Merge two preference dictionaries without mutating the original.
+
+    This function creates a deep copy of ``defaults`` and then updates it with ``overrides``.
+    Nested mutable structures (e.g., dictionaries and lists) are also copied to
+    prevent side effects.
+    """
+    # Create a deep copy of defaults to avoid altering the original data
+    merged = deepcopy(defaults)
+
+    # Merge notifications â€“ both the defaults and overrides may provide this key
+    default_notifications = merged.setdefault("notifications", {})
+    overrides_notifications = overrides.get("notifications", {})
+    default_notifications.update(overrides_notifications)
+
+    # Merge tags â€“ similar to notifications but with list operations
+    default_tags = merged.setdefault("tags", [])
+    overrides_tags = overrides.get("tags", [])
+    default_tags.extend(overrides_tags)
+
+    # Copy any additional topâ€‘level keys from overrides
     for key, value in overrides.items():
         if key not in {"notifications", "tags"}:
             merged[key] = value
+
     return merged
